data.title: Aristotle Focus Growth Q2 Highlights: Top Performers, Surprises And Strategic Shifts
data.description:
The following segment was excerpted from the Aristotle Focus Growth Q2 2025 Commentary.

Contributors and Detractors for 2Q 2025

Relative Contributors Relative Detractors
Adaptive Biotechnologies (ADPT) UnitedHealth Group (UNH)
Netflix (NFLX)

Visa (V)
Trane Technologies (TT) Bio-Techne (TECH)
Broadcom (AVGO) S&P Global (SPGI)
CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) Costco Wholesale (COST)

Detractors

UnitedHealth Group

UnitedHealth Group detracted from relative performance in the second quarter following a surprisingly soft first quarter earnings report and a sizable guidance reduction. The issues were two-fold, including higher utilization inside its group Medicare Advantage offering, which drove a higher medical care ratio. The second issue was a negative new member mix, as several competitors exited the market, and many of the patients who switched to UnitedHealth’s offerings had not been actively engaged in their care, resulting in higher acuity levels throughout the quarter. The company believes both issues are fixable in its next rate cycle.

Visa

Visa detracted from relative performance in the second quarter due to growing concerns that stablecoins could pose a long-term threat to the company’s payments business. While consumer adoption of stablecoins remains limited at present, pending U.S. legislation viewed as favorable to the cryptocurrency ecosystem, along with interest from large merchants exploring the issuance of proprietary stablecoins, has highlighted potential disruption risks to parts of Visa’s core network.

Contributors

Adaptive Biotechnologies

Adaptive Biotechnologies contributed to relative performance in the second quarter following a better-than-expected first quarter earnings report, which showed solid across-the-board momentum in both clinical testing and biopharma testing volumes. Adaptive continues to make progress on growing average selling prices (ASPS) and reducing cash burn. Adaptive is also rolling out its test on electronic medical record platforms, which will simplify the workflow and increase test ordering, processing and reporting efficiency.

Netflix

Netflix contributed to relative performance in the second quarter, primarily due to first quarter earnings results that supported investor confidence in continued growth. In particular, additional price increases in select markets, healthy subscriber momentum and progress in the nascent ad-supported subscription tier also helped drive performance. The company reiterated its full-year targets, with no signs of weakness in domestic or international markets.

Recent Portfolio Activity

The table below shows all buys and sells completed during the quarter, followed by a brief rationale.

Buys Sells
HubSpot (HUBS) Expedia Group (EXPE)

Buys

HubSpot

HubSpot provides a cloud-based customer relationship management (CRM) platform that integrates marketing, sales, service, content management and operations hubs into a unified system. Its platform features applications and tools for tasks such as website creation, business blogging, search engine optimization, web analytics, lead generation and more, while also offering a native payment solution. Designed to create adaptable and cohesive customer experiences, HubSpot’s Smart CRM and engagement hubs facilitate unified customer profiles and seamless interaction across the customer lifecycle. The hubs, available in free and paid tiers, can function independently, integrate with HubSpot’s Smart platform or third-party CRMs, and scale alongside growing businesses.

We invested in HubSpot for its strategic positioning in a large, underserved SMB market, its effective freemium-to-enterprise conversion funnel and its rapidly growing AI suite “Breeze,” which enhances automation and personalization across customer interactions. With strong adoption across product tiers, momentum in enterprise expansion, and scalable financials driving margin expansion and free cash flow growth, we believe the company is well-positioned to deliver durable, long-term value. We believe the current valuation is fair based on the combination of durable revenue growth, expanding margins and the early signs of traction in AI monetization.

Sells

Expedia Group

We sold Expedia Group due to a higher risk of a recession following the significant increase in global tariffs, which can be expected to reduce overall levels of economic activity. In addition, the negative impact on the wealth effect due to potentially declining equity markets will likely impact demand for retail travel. Travel is among the most discretionary spend categories within the consumer discretionary sector.

data.title: Stocks That Shaped Miller's Q2 2025 Deep Value Strategy
data.description:
The following segment was excerpted from the Miller Deep Value Select Strategy Q2 2025 Letter

During the quarter, our largest contributor was Gannett (GCI) up 24%, recovering a portion of its first quarter drawdown. Gannett remains significantly mispriced in our opinion at only .25x revenue and >50% normalized free cash flow yield. New York Times (NYT), which went through a similar digital transformation ten years ago, has a much higher valuation level, with a price-to-sales of 3x and Enterprise Value to EBITDA (EV/EBITDA) greater than 15x. Our second largest positive contributors was Tutor Perini (TPC), whose shares were up 83%. We initially purchased TPC in the Fall of 2022, near $7/share, a deep discount to tangible book value. The Covid outbreak caused a significant delay in larger civil contracts, weighing on the company’s most profitable segment margins, causing a multi-year earnings trough. Over the past two years, management has successfully rebuilt the company pipeline, enhanced the balance sheet through significant debt reduction and is on track to return the company to normalized profitability. With a portfolio holding return over 400%, TPC is currently trading over two times book value and approaching 10x normalized earnings. We recently exited the position and have recycled capital adding to holdings that have recently underperformed the portfolio and have greater long-term embedded return potential.

Our two largest detractors during the quarter were JELD-WEN (JELD) and Nabors Industries (NBR) and that were down 33% and 32% during the quarter, respectively. Both companies’ share prices are at deep discounts to what we believe is their long-term fundamental value, and we increased position sizes in both holdings during the quarter.

JELD-WEN is a leading North American and European manufacturer and distributor of interior and exterior doors and windows to the new construction and remodeling sectors.

The share price continued to be under pressure due to the ongoing housing weakness impacting company revenues and profitability. At the end of Q1, management took additional cost actions, which should amount to annual savings of $50M, along with $100M of estimated additional productivity savings for the year. These transformation initiatives have not yet caught up with revenue weakness, as company EBITDA margins are currently at historical trough levels. We expect to see EBITDA margins stabilize and improve over the coming 6-12 months.

JELD-WEN’s multi-year transformation plan has a lot of self-help initiatives, having already reduced the fixed cost structure by $350M over the past couple of years. As the company rationalizes the manufacturing and distribution network, there could be opportunities for additional non-core asset monetization overtime. We see similarities to the Masonite transformation plan 10 years ago. Masonite was able to improve EBITDA margins from single digits to 20% before being sold to Owens Corning (GLW) at 8.6x EV/EBITDA.

Near-term risk is a deeper housing trough that further impacts the remodeling marketplace, causing greater near-term revenue weakness and slower recovery in company margins. The company has ample liquidity, approximately $500M of unused credit facility and greater than $100M+ of cash on balance sheet. We believe that long-term upside potential from JELD-WEN transformation plan supports double-digit normalized margins. With greater than 50% normalized earnings and free cash flow yield, we believe JELD’s share price is significantly mispriced and has upside potential multiples of the current price level.

Nabors Industries (NBR) has undertaken a multi-year transformation focused on leveraging their industry’s leading technology. The company has over 450 patents, and has developed industry leading drilling automation/robotics, Rig Cloud platform and smart apps, rolling out globally, not only on Nabors rigs but also on third-party rigs. The company’s recent Parker Wellbore acquisition more than doubles the Nabors Drilling Solutions (NDS) segment, while further expanding their digital offerings and accelerating the transformation plan. Nabors NDS segment has very low capital intensity with 90% annual free cash flow conversion. As NDS becomes a larger portion of the business over time it should further accelerate future free cash flow generation and debt reduction.

With the recent share price weakness, Nabors looks extremely attractive, at less than one times forward cash flow – the only other period it has traded lower was a short time during the Covid 2020 outbreak. Current marketplace fears are due to falling commodity prices, and expectations for additional rig cancellations later in the year. While oil prices falling towards $50/barrel is a potential risk, we believe this marketplace fear is well known and significantly discounted in Nabors current share price today. Nabors hopes to realize at least $60M in annual merger synergies from the recently completed acquisition, and $200M+ of annual free cash flow generation and accelerated debt reduction.

Nabors’ business segments are all currently trading at a significant valuation discount to their peers. Peer valuation multiples for each of their business segments appears to support an Enterprise Value much greater than the current valuation. In this situation, there are multiple paths to improvement and value creation; we don’t necessarily need all business segments to reach peer valuation multiples given the significant overall discount today. Nabors market cap today is only $540M.

Success on the transformation plan should materially increase Nabors equity market capitalization. We believe Nabors’ long-term normalized EBITDA could be closer to $1.5B, and the company has debt reduction potential of $1B+ over the next five years. Nabors looks significantly mispriced at greater than 50% normalized free cash flow yield.

11) Nifty Fifty – late 1972, 2) Dot Com Bubble – early 2000 (March), and 3) COVID peak – early 2020 (February).

The US Policy Uncertainty Index measures policy-related economic uncertainty using three underlying components: news coverage, federal tax code provisions, and the Federal Reserve’s Survey of Professional Forescasters. For more information, please visit Economic Policy Uncertainty Index. The S&P 500 Index (SP500, SPX) is a market capitalization-weighted index of 500 widely held common stocks. Price to sales ratio is a tool for calculating a stock’s valuation relative to other companies. It is calculated by dividing a stock’s current price by its revenue per share. The Russell 2000® Index (RTY) is a small-cap stock market index that makes up the smallest 2,000 stocks in the Russell 3000 Index. The Russell 1000® Index measures the performance of the 1,000 largest companies in the Russell 3000 Index, which represents approximately 92% of the total market capitalization of the Russell 3000 Index. The Russell 2000® Value Index measures the performance of the smallcap value segment of the US equity universe. The Russell Midcap® Value Index measures the performance of the midcap value segment of the US equity universe. The Russell Microcap® Value Index measures the performance of the microcap value segment of the US equity market. Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) is a valuation methodology used when assessing diversified companies or conglomerates with multiple distinct business segments. PF Adjusted EBITDA (Pro Forma Adjusted Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) is a variation of Adjusted EBITDA that includes further adjustments, often to reflect the impact of acquisitions or other significant events as if they had occurred at the beginning of a specific period. Peer multiples are EV/EBITDA. EV at Midpoint reflects the pro-forma adjusted EBITDA on the left and multiplying it by the midpoint of the “peer multiples” listed, to get what the implied enterprise value would be at given those EBITDA. EBITDA is earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization and is a calculation of a company’s financial health. Enterprise value (EV) is a measure of a company’s total value. Tangible book value (TBV) is the value of a company’s tangible assets minus its liabilities and intangible assets. Book value is the value at which an asset is carried on a balance sheet. Normalized free cash flow yield looks over a 10-year cycle at normalized margins and capital expenditures to determine normalized free cash flow power of the of assets divided by the current market cap. Normalized EBITDA is a company’s Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA), adjusted to remove the effects of unusual, non-recurring, or one-time events. Free cash flow is earnings before depreciation, amortization, and non-cash charges minus maintenance capital expenditures. Normalized earnings yield smoothes out company earnings over a ten year cycle to give the sense of the true earnings power of the asset base. Cyclical companies will have trough margins/earnings at the trough of the 10 year cycle and peak margins/earnings at the peak of the cycle. Forward cash flow refers to the projected or anticipated cash inflows and outflows of a business over a future period. Free cash flow (FCF) conversion measures how effectively a company turns its earnings into usable cash. It indicates the percentage of a company’s EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) that is converted into free cash flow.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan: A Large Producer With More Potential Downside
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) is one of the largest copper producers in the world, worth almost $60 billion. The company has slightly underperformed a strong market since we recommended against investing in a market with massive copper uncertainty. With the company's latest earnings and tariff changes, we see the company as continuing to be a poor investment.

Freeport-McMoRan's 2Q Results

Freeport-McMoRan saw $1.13 in unit net cash costs for the quarter; however, it saw $1.56 in cash costs for 1H 2025.

The company has continued to sell massive amounts of copper, roughly 1 billion pounds/quarter, while continuing to benefit from high gold prices in the market. FCX commenced start-up at its new Indonesian smelter, which hasn't been factored into the company's CAPEX. The company's shareholder returns have been very modest, at roughly 2% annualized.

FCX has continued to invest heavily, and as a result, its FCF remains low. Annualized FCF for 1H 2025 is $3.6 billion, a 6.3% FCF yield. That's a relatively low yield in a capex-intensive industry with volatile prices.

The company's largest center of operations is Indonesia, where it has negative net cash costs as a result of its massive gold production from the Grasberg mine. The company also has substantial operations in South America, where Chile is the largest copper producer. The company's unit cash costs of $2.46/pound are reasonable.

In the United States, the company's operations are even more expensive. The company stood to benefit massively from announced tariffs on copper into the United States; however, as we'll discuss below, that seems to have been reverted.

Copper Prices and Tariffs

The U.S. government is currently investigating copper with a variety of tariff proposals.

The initial announcement was a 50% tariff on all copper imports, which put a massive premium on COMEX (U.S. copper prices). However, with the goal to support domestic refining, the tariffs were then updated so that they only applied to semi-finished products. The result was a massive decline in the spread back to 2024 levels of almost 0.

Given this response to the tariffs and the fact that the update is mainly to protect domestic capacity, we see this as not a major source of income for Freeport-McMoRan.

Freeport-McMoRan's Returns

Freeport-McMoRan is focused on driving returns, but the company's ability to do so versus its valuation is difficult.

The company's 2026/2027 operating cash flow guidance at no premium at $4.5/pound of copper is roughly $9 billion. At current copper prices of $4.4/pound, that has dropped down to below $9 billion. It's also worth noting that copper prices are at multi-year highs, and in the event of a major downturn, they could decline substantially.

It's worth noting that from 2022 to mid-2024, copper prices were less than $4 per pound. Immediately after COVID-19, they dropped to almost $2 per pound.

The company is spending massive amounts of capital despite a lack of major growth within its production. The company's 2025-2026 capital guidance is for $4.4 billion annualized, of which $2.8 billion is the minimal and discretionary capital is more than $1.6 billion. The company needs to maintain this spending to keep its production strong.

However, with roughly $9 billion in operating cash flow at current high copper prices, that's still just over $4 billion in annual FCF. It's respectable, but it's not enough to justify an almost $60 billion valuation.

The company has pushed its net debt to near 0, but shareholder returns have been weak. Since mid-2021, a 4-year period, the company has only driven roughly 10% in total shareholder returns. That's been split among its base and variable dividends, along with share repurchases. It also avoids a lot of the immediate impact of COVID-19 that the company has faced.

It shows the impact of volatility in copper prices along with the company's capital-intensive industry. Even if you add the $2 billion in balance sheet improvement, you'd be at 13%, not much better. It's just over 3% in annualized shareholder returns.

Thesis Risk

The largest risk to our thesis is something that boosts copper prices substantially. That could be the tariffs, or it could be massive infrastructure spending, for example, a global policy to solve climate change and replace electric infrastructure at a much quicker rate. New electric infrastructure for artificial intelligence also has a major impact.

All of this could improve Freeport-McMoRan's cash flow and ability to drive returns substantially.

Conclusion

Freeport-McMoRan has an impressive portfolio of assets, and the company is especially well positioned with the low-cost Grasberg Mine and U.S. assets that could have protectionist tariffs. However, those tariffs seem to have been brushed back, and the company trades at an incredibly lofty valuation of almost $60 billion.

Over the past 4 years, the company's total shareholder returns have only been 12%, counting balance sheet improvements. The company hasn't invested massively or dramatically grown production during that time. The company is currently in a high copper price environment, but there's no guarantee that it will continue, and that makes the company a poor long-term investment.

Let us know your thoughts in the comments below.

data.title: Wasatch Micro Cap Value Fund Q2 2025 Commentary
data.description:
Overview

U.S. micro cap stocks ended the quarter with sharp gains. Initially, stocks turned lower after President Trump unveiled tariff rates for countries that were much higher than investors had anticipated. However, equities then rebounded sharply as the President’s economic team appeared to be softening its stance on tariffs.

The shifting view on tariffs created a “risk-on” environment, sending most micro cap stocks up sharply. For the second quarter, the Russell Microcap® Index finished up 15.51%. The Wasatch Micro Cap Value Fund—Investor Class was up 9.84%, but trailed the benchmark.

The Fund performed largely as we would have expected given the risk-on backdrop. Our investment focus is on high-quality businesses. Given that focus, we expect to outperform the benchmark in uncertain environments or when markets are down. As these companies grow long-term earnings and demonstrate the high-quality nature of their businesses, we expect these stocks to outperform over longer time horizons. But in a fast-rising, risk-on environment, we generally have found that the stocks of some lower-quality companies with more speculative business models have tended to outperform. Our low exposure to such businesses has hurt performance relative to the benchmark.

Additionally, we held a couple of companies that experienced perceived hiccups, and those stocks weighed on performance. Those detractors are discussed in further detail in the section below.

Details of the quarter

The largest detractor from the Fund’s performance was Champion Homes, Inc. (SKY). The company is one of the largest homebuilders in North America, specializing in modular homes for single-family, multi-family and hospitality segments. Champion’s stock was down after company management reported weaker than expected quarterly operating results. High interest rates make home payments more expensive, so some of Champion’s customers are offsetting that expense by opting out of some of the higher-grade amenities within modular houses. This is a headwind for Champion’s earnings because those amenities tend to improve margins for the company. The short- term results haven’t changed our view of the company. In an environment where homebuilders face significant challenges, we were pleased to see Champion increase revenue 10.7% in its most recent quarter over the same period a year ago.

Axogen, Inc. (AXGN) also detracted. The company specializes in providing surgical solutions to restore nerve function and quality of life for patients with nerve damage. Axogen’s stock was down after the company missed earnings and management announced an inventory writedown. We believe the writedown was largely due to some customers waiting for the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to approve Axogen’s biologics license application for its nerve graft solution. But over the long term, we believe Axogen’s technology will be widely adopted by surgeons.

Weave Communications, Inc. (WEAV) was another detractor. The company provides vertical software solutions to dentists and specialty medical practices. The company is making investments to grow the business, and those costs will crimp earnings. This weighed on the stock during the period, but we think management made the right decision to invest in the firm’s growth. We expect to see those investments gain traction over the next 12 months. We added to our position size when the stock was down.

Limbach Holdings, Inc. (LMB) was the top contributor to performance. The company is a heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) services company whose clients typically include large institutions such as hospitals, medical campuses and universities. Over the prior three years, Limbach has undergone a transition that makes it a general contractor, as opposed to a subcontractor, in many of its customer relationships. Serving as a general contractor allows Limbach to have stronger relationships with clients, and helps to improve margins. We believed 2025 would be an important year for the firm, during which most of that transition would be completed and would be reflected in rising revenues and profits. That has happened so far this year and the stock has responded accordingly.

Another large contributor was Graham Corp. (GHM), a defense contractor specializing in propulsion and vacuum-related products. A large order from the U.S. Navy lifted Graham’s recent operating results and drove the stock higher. Looking ahead, we believe Graham will continue to benefit from increased defense spending. Additionally, we like Graham’s potential earnings stability. Programs to build new ships often run for a decade or longer, and once a company’s products are incorporated into the design of a ship, it’s unlikely that those products will be displaced by a competitor. This can create a durable, long-lasting revenue stream for a company like Graham.

NPK International, Inc. (NPKI) also contributed. The company is a leading provider of site access solutions, offering sustainable composite matting products. Utility and energy companies use such products to operate heavy machinery in wet environments. When we initiated our investment in the stock, we classified NPK as an “undiscovered grower.” NPK had divested a lower-margin fluids business, and in our view, many investors hadn’t realized what that divestment could mean for the company’s earnings power. Now, those changes are leading to better profitability and improved returns on invested capital (ROIC) for the firm. The stock has risen as investors have taken note. (Current and future holdings are subject to risk and change.)

Outlook and positioning

As of the end of June, there remained a great deal of uncertainty about tariffs and their effects on the U.S. economy. Given the lack of clarity, we wouldn’t be surprised if markets stay volatile, with stocks trading based on the latest positive or negative news about the issue. However, tariff headlines have little effect on how we manage the Fund, and we feel positive about the stocks we’re holding.

Just as we’ve done every quarter, we’ve reviewed the fundamentals and earnings models of all our companies. What was striking about our review this quarter was the low number of businesses in which fundamentals were deteriorating or the stock thesis was in question. In fact, we believe there are fewer portfolio companies with questionable earnings potential than at just about any point we can remember.

As we look across the Fund, we believe we’ve assembled a collection of high-quality, competitively advantaged businesses, with good management teams that should be able to navigate tariffs, a potential economic slowdown, or any other operational headwind that comes their way. Over the long term, we believe strong fundamentals for these companies will lead the Fund to outperform its benchmark.

Thank you for the opportunity to manage your assets.

Sincerely,

Thomas Bradley, Gene Robin and Brian Bythrow

data.title: Why B2Gold Could Be Gold's Smartest Bet In 2025: Valuation Meets Resilience
data.description:
In a market where opportunities are rarer than gold, B2Gold Corp. (BTG) is an attractive opportunity supported by strong fundamentals, clear growth drivers, and a valuation that currently appears disconnected from the market. With gold at unprecedented highs, and the company in various stages of expanding operations, BTG is a very compelling investment opportunity within the gold market.

B2Gold is at the most consequential juncture in its corporate history, as the commissioning of the Goose mine signals potential for stock price appreciation, as it both diversifies the company’s exposure to more stable jurisdictions and is expected to increase total production by 20–33% by year-end 2025

The current market is optimal for gold producers like B2Gold, with the metal at unprecedented highs and analyst estimates projecting prices of $4000 by mid-2026. The company is benefitting from volume expansion and gold price expansion to create a multiplier effect on cash flows that the current statistics simply aren't

About The Company And Its Value Creation

The operating portfolio represents a geographical diversification that helps mitigate risk and provide upside. The company's primary operating assets include:

  • Fekola Mine: This is the company's flagship asset located in Mali and produced 392,946 ounces in 2024 (it is also possible that there is some upside potential to produce more ounces by optimizing underground expansions and processing higher grades of ore).
  • Masbate Mine: The overall performance from the Philippines operation returned 194,046 ounces on a yearly basis while exceeding expectations and while still providing competitive margins.
  • Otjikoto Mine: This Namibian mine is a consistent and preferred producer while returning 198,142 ounces in the 2024 year and completing its exposure with the portfolio of African market optionality.
  • Goose Project: Located in Canada, it is the key factor for the growth and risk diversification because it is the first production that began in June 2025, marking the company’s first operational presence in Canada. Goose Mine is not another mine, it changes the entire risk profile and provides an opportunity for expansion. Goose Mine is located in the Back River Gold Belt in Nunavut, and there is legal certainty there, and the cash flows are extremely high. The overview is like this: The mine is expected to produce approximately 120,000-150,000 ounces for the remainder of 2025 after June, when operations began. It is expected to produce about 310,000 ounces annually from 2026-2031. The operation is expected to have a nine year effective life.

Competition And Competitive Position

The fact is, B2Gold shows strong relative positioning in the gold mining industry , and with performance that validates its strategy. They have low operating costs, a genuinely diversified global asset base, and there’s real momentum in their growth pipeline. When you assess their peers, in all candor, they look like they’re built to last.

  • Eldorado Gold (EGO) is up 43% in 2025, which grabs headlines, but dig into the numbers and B2Gold offers more attractive valuation metrics. The forward P/E for B2Gold sits at 6.1x, compare that to EGO’s 13.4x. That’s a massive gap, and it suggests B2Gold is much better value for investors right now.
  • Equinox Gold (EQX): EQX isn’t much different, its P/E sits at 10.78x, still way above B2Gold’s. Plus, B2Gold beats EQX on asset quality and global reach.
  • IAMGOLD (IAG): Despite the great rally that IAG had in 2025 of more than 35%, we see that B2Gold's metrics are equally superior, with much higher operational efficiency and a much more diversified risk profile.

The strong competitive position that the company has is based on three pillars: the first is its very low operating costs, with cash costs as low as $835–$895 per ounce and AISC between $1,460–$1,520/oz for 2025; the second is its great geographical diversification in different jurisdictions that significantly reduces risk; and the third is a growth pipeline that few competitors will be able to match.

Financial Analysis

B2Gold's financial fundamentals represent a more compelling value narrative than the market has yet to recognize. With the price of gold at an all-time high at $3,357 per ounce, the divergence between the stock's price and the stock's value has widened, strongly supporting SA's Quant STRONG BUY rating.

A forward P/E ratio of 6.1x, representing a 62% discount relative to the sector average of 16x, stands out as a significant outlier, especially given current gold prices, which are holding above $3300/oz. Citi’s forecast has the metal climbing to around $3500/oz in the next three months, adding further context to just how unusual this valuation is.

Currently, the company’s forward EV/EBITDA sits at 2.58x, remarkably low, given these margins. For context, the sector average hovers around 9x, so we’re looking at a discount of roughly 71%. Such a pronounced valuation gap is highly unusual in developed markets and points to a clear pricing anomaly.

Applying the current gold prices makes the Seeking Alpha Quant rating more important.

Its valuation at A is short, as with gold at that price, the discount is not only evident but historically extreme, since with its EV/EBITDA at that level for a company that generates margins of approximately 30%, appears significantly undervalued relative to peers and fundamentals.

With this data, we see how total liquidity is extremely high and net debt over EBITDA is very low, which gives it an ultra-conservative position and a great capacity for leverage if needed in the future. This level of solidity is extremely rare in the gold mining sector and is very defensive against any adverse price cycles.

Regarding its momentum, in a market where uncertainty is constantly present, safe-haven assets like gold are becoming increasingly representative in investors' portfolios, so in the next moments of a possible recession or large-scale crisis, the price of gold would rise exponentially, greatly benefiting the company's price.

The so-called “gold rally” isn’t just another cyclical uptick, it’s more like a reflection of underlying shifts in the global economic landscape that are starting to favor precious metals as reliable stores of value.

We have aggressive monetary expansion, ballooning sovereign debt, and geopolitical fragmentation, all of which bode pretty well for gold. In this circumstance, gold shines as a hedge against currency dilution and offers useful diversification for portfolios. Get into the underlying structural issues here: the ongoing geopolitical uncertainty keeps risk elevated; this is driving many institutional investors to view gold as a hedge against inflation.

Additionally, protectionist trade policies are a strain on the dollar, and, around the world, central banks, particularly in emerging markets, have been actively ramping up gold purchasing. All these factors combine to create demand and an underpin for gold, which altogether makes this much more than just another temporary rally.

With these fundamentals, we could apply multiples of 8.5x EV/EBITDA, which is their low median in the sector, and we could see the following projections:

  • With gold at the current price, the intrinsic value per share would be around $2.2, with an upside potential of +255%.
  • If gold were at $3,500 an ounce, as Citi estimates, the intrinsic value of the stock would be around $3.1, which implies an upside potential of +282%.

These figures could even be considered prudent given the current context, due to the high operational leverage offered by the gold price and the peak production of key assets like Goose.

Technical Analysis

In the chart, we can see how, after making a pullback on the wedge started in August 2020, B2Gold has made a strong bounce supported by a significant volume increase. In the long term, the price had support very exactly in the zone of $2.20, which also matches the 0.5 Fibonacci level. From that level of major importance, the value rebuilt upwards, altering the former pattern of lower highs and lows and printing a new higher high, indicating a probable change in the underlying trend.

Following the above pullback, the price has returned to the zone of its recent highs, now coinciding with the 0.236 Fibo level. After presenting a slowdown there, it might be hoped that the value will continue with the upward path, thereby consolidating the reversal of the trend that was starting to be intuited in that price structure. From a technical standpoint, three main resistance levels to watch appear: the levels of $4.30, $5.70, and $7.50. Overcoming these barriers would pave the way for a free rise, with the potential to reach the target levels derived from fundamental analysis.

Also, the stochastic indicates the value has moved out of the overbought area but continues to retain a strong bullish bias in favor of the anticipation of a further price increase. In summary, the structural and technical reading is extremely positive and, when coupled with a good fundamental analysis, supports the logical expectation of further new highs for B2Gold over the medium to long term.

Catalysts

The ramp-up of the Goose mine: June was a significant operational milestone with the beginning of production at this mine. It is predicted to be 120,000 to 150,000 ounces for the rest of the year as per forecast. It is predicted to produce 310,000 ounces per year between 2026 and 2032. This improvement increases not only total production, but also acts to reduce asset configurational risks.

The expansion and optimization of the Fekola mine: This flagship Malian operation offers considerable upside from both the development of underground deposits and high-grade ore, which could provide production and margin expansion through to 2026.

Gold price: As gold is trading above $3300 an ounce, this makes B2Gold a clear winner since it directly gains from the metal's revaluation.

Operational efficiency improvement: Minimization of the costs and their optimization in operations, together with other sustainable technologies, lead to enhanced financial performance.

Risks

The strongest operation presently for the company is Fekola, a nation that has political and regulatory issues that may affect production and raise costs. Any decline in the stability of the nation or any adverse regulatory changes may have a negative effect on the company and its performance.

Risks in Goose launch: Though representatively it is a source of growth, it is an early-stage project, and hence any operating problems may impact the anticipated cash flow and market confidence.

Volatility of gold prices: While most forecasts lean positive for gold prices, let’s not kid ourselves, global economic shifts and changes in monetary policy can still cause some serious swings. That kind of volatility can take a toll on B2Gold’s overall stability.

Financial and capital risks: Also, the need for continuous investment to expand and maintain the Goose isn’t exactly pocket change. The financial structure and cash flow may be seriously strained if things go wrong, whether that be due to a volatile global economy or unforeseen policy changes. From a business standpoint, it's not exactly ideal.

Conclusion

B2Gold stands out as a compelling player in the gold sector right now. The company’s diversified portfolio and strategic assets put it in a strong position, even as the broader market faces plenty of challenges. With the Goose mine now operational and ongoing optimization at Fekola, B2Gold is set for a notable uptick in production, without taking on unnecessary risk.

In addition, the company, which is highly leveraged to the gold price, has some of its highest margins ever, which means strong cash flows and a healthy balance sheet. This is not really a sight you see on a daily basis, especially in this market.

Against an environment of continued economic and geopolitical challenges, B2Gold continues to differentiate itself with a disciplined business diversification strategy and stringent financial controls. This ensures the stability of the company when its market conditions become turbulent. Those investors who seek exposure to the gold sector at a relative discount will find B2Gold well positioned to offer both downside protection and real growth in the current landscape.

The following segment was excerpted from the Baron Real Estate Fund Q2 2025 Shareholder Letter.

Top Contributors and Detractors

Top contributors to performance for the quarter

Quarter End Market Cap($B) Contribution to Return(%)
Brookfield (BN) Corporation 102.1 1.02
Wynn Resorts, Limited (WYNN) 9.8 0.51
Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (BAM) 90.7 0.45
Toll Brothers, Inc. (TOL) 11.2 0.41
CBRE Group, Inc. (CBRE) 41.8 0.37

Brookfield Corporation is a leading global owner and operator of real assets such as real estate and infrastructure. We believe the company’s global reach, large scale capital, and the synergies among its businesses provide significant opportunities for growth. While shares performed well in the quarter, we continue to believe the company offers significant value at a recent share price of $62. Brookfield’s management team, who in our opinion is credible and conservative, believes the company is worth $100 per share today and $176 in five years. Brookfield has investments in publicly traded and private real estate-related businesses. Brookfield’s ownership interests in four publicly listed Brookfield companies (Brookfield Asset Management, Brookfield Infrastructure Partners, Brookfield Renewable, and Brookfield Business Partners) are currently valued in the public market at $52 per share or almost the same price as the share price for the entire company. The public market is currently ascribing little value to Brookfield’s non-public investments, insurance business and carried interest, which we believe are also worth at least $25 per share (net of debt) today and offer significant further upside through growth.

Wynn Resorts, Limited is the pre-eminent luxury global owner and operator of integrated resorts (hotels and casino resorts). The company has developed “best-in-class” real estate assets in Las Vegas (Wynn and Encore), Boston (Encore Boston Harbor), and Macau (Wynn Macau and Wynn Palace). The company continues to invest in its hotel and casino assets to maintain its lead in each market.

We are also bullish on the prospects for the company’s newest development - the Wynn Al Marjan Island in the UAE (expected to open early in 2027). We believe the UAE is the most exciting new market for integrated resort developments in decades.

We believe the valuation of Wynn remains compelling and reflects limited growth in Las Vegas, Macau, and Boston, and largely ignores the growth that we expect from its UAE development.

Wynn’s management agrees that its shares are highly discounted as they have been allocating incremental capital toward share repurchases. In addition, Tilman Fertitta, a highly successful hotel, casino, and entertainment executive, has acquired more than $1 billion of Wynn shares (including $150 million in his most recent March 2025 purchase) and is now the largest shareholder of Wynn with a 12% stake in the company.

Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is the asset management arm that was spun out from Brookfield Corporation in December 2022. Brookfield was a contributor to performance due to continued strong fundraising in a volatile macro and geopolitical backdrop along with improving margins and fee-related earnings growth. Brookfield Asset Management is a leading global alternative asset manager with approximately $1 trillion of assets under management across renewable power & transition, infrastructure, private equity, real estate and credit. We had previously written about and had consistently believed that the captive asset manager was undervalued when it was housed within the broader Brookfield organization. The tax-free spin-out to existing holders simplified the organization and allowed shareholders to access a steady earnings stream underpinned by base management fees (versus highly variable performance fees) that have a high degree of growth visibility. The market has come to our view with the shares of Brookfield Asset Management trading in-line or a slight premium to other alternative asset managers. We had argued for a premium valuation given our view that Brookfield Asset Management would be able to grow its fee-related earnings per share in the upper teens for the foreseeable future. We continue to believe shares are attractively valued given the multi-year earnings outlook for the Company.

Top detractors from performance for the quarter

Quarter End Market Cap or Market Cap When Sold ($B) Contribution to Return (%)
Churchill Downs Incorporated (CHDN) 6.6 (0.27)
Prologis, Inc. (PLD) 99.8 (0.22)
Independence Realty Trust, Inc. (IRT) 4.1 (0.20)
Louisiana-Pacific Corporation (LPX) 6.0 (0.16)
Equinix, Inc. (EQIX) 77.8 (0.15)

Churchill Downs Incorporated, which has acquired and developed various entertainment assets including the Churchill Downs racetrack which hosts the Kentucky Derby, was a detractor during the quarter following an earnings release that showed weakening trends in their Virginia market and lower-than-expected Kentucky Derby earnings. The company also indefinitely delayed a large project to expand the Kentucky Derby, but it was not clear about pivoting available capital to buying-back shares. We believe the Virginia market and their regional gaming portfolio may remain under pressure. The company has numerous growth projects with attractive prospects, but developments will take time to ramp to full profitability and in the meantime leverage remains high. Due to uncertain business trends and concerns around competitive pressures in their Virginia market, we decided to exit the position.

The shares of Prologis, Inc., a best-in-class industrial REIT, underperformed in the second quarter due to a somewhat slower leasing environment (following a robust first quarter) as many companies paused lease decision making while awaiting more clarity on global tariffs and trade policies. Our sense is that leasing activity has still improved from the beginning of the year and that significant pent-up demand could be unleashed as business confidence improves.

We recently had the pleasure of hosting CEO Hamid Moghadam for a meeting in our office, and we were left with the following takeaways:

  1. A reminder that Prologis benefits from a high-quality real estate portfolio, an unmatched global platform, strong competitive advantages (scale, data, and technology), and an exceptional management team.
  2. Hamid remains optimistic about the multi-year prospects for Prologis, predicated on a compelling multi-year outlook for demand/supply/rent growth, significant embedded growth potential from in-place rents that are generally 30% below market rents, and several secular demand tailwinds (e-commerce, supply chain logistics, more inventory safety stock, nearshoring/onshoring).
  3. Hamid expects that Prologis’s recent foray into select data center development (that carry high risk-adjusted returns on already owned land) will likely become an increasingly accretive source of growth in the coming years.

We continue to believe the appreciation potential for Prologis’ shares remains compelling given the strong runway for future cash flow and earnings growth in the next several years and a more favorable valuation multiple following a recent correct in share price.

Independence Realty Trust, Inc. (IRT) was a detractor during the quarter due to economic concerns of tariff policies impacting lower income consumers, softer-than-expected pricing power on new leases, and mixed investor reception of the company’s select acquisitions. IRT owns 33,000 apartment units that cater to a more affordable income demographic. We believe the return prospects for the stock continue to be attractive given the company’s discounted public market valuation relative to both recent private market transactions and publicly traded peers that have communities in overlapping markets, its “value-add” program that provides enhanced growth prospects versus peers, abating supply deliveries in its markets that should enhance pricing power and its superior management team.

data.title: What Baron Bought And Sold In Q2: Airbnb, BXP, And More
data.description:
The following segment was excerpted from the Baron Real Estate Fund Q2 2025 Shareholder Letter.

Top net purchases for the quarter

Quarter End Market Cap($B) Net Amount Purchased($M)
Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) 83.1 40.6
BXP, Inc. (BXP) 10.7 32.5
Equinix, Inc. (EQIX) 77.8 30.4
Toll Brothers, Inc. (TOL) 11.2 28.9
Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) 52.5 23.9

During the most recent quarter, we initiated a new position in Airbnb, Inc. We have been following and meeting with the company since its IPO in 2020 and took advantage of the indiscriminate sell-off in April to buy a high-quality company that was on sale with a favorable risk/reward setup under a wide range of economic scenarios. Airbnb is the largest technology-enabled hospitality platform in the world with 8 million listings and 5 million hosts across 220 countries and 100,000 cities. We are optimistic regarding the multi-year prospects for Airbnb due to: i) its leading share in alternative accommodations and scale driving strong brand awareness and repeat bookings; ii) 90% direct traffic allowing for lower customer acquisition costs; iii) its strong value proposition to both guests and hosts, leading to differentiated listings and exclusive inventory; iv) a unique two-sided marketplace of user reviews curating an ecosystem of trust between guests and hosts; and, v) imbedded free call options with the company’s recently launched experiences, services, and other incremental products to be introduced in the coming years (e.g. longer-term stay apartments, host services, partnerships).

During the quarter we acquired shares of BXP, Inc. (formerly known as Boston Properties), a blue-chip office REIT that owns a portfolio of premier office properties concentrated in coastal U.S. markets including Boston, New York City, San Francisco, Washington, DC, Los Angeles, and Seattle. We are excited about BXP’s prospects for several reasons:

  1. Blue-chip company, with an irreplaceable portfolio in markets with high barriers to entry, a proven development track record, a strong balance sheet, and an excellent management team.
  2. Strong fundamental backdrop for high-quality office properties in select markets. Generally, return-to-work and a pick-up in lease decision-making has led to improved leasing velocity across many office markets, particularly for high-quality properties that are benefitting from a “flight to quality.” At the same time, the supply picture for many office markets is favorable over the next several years given a dearth of new construction deliveries. We believe this backdrop can support improved rent growth in many markets over the next several years.
  3. BXP anticipates a growth inflection in 2026, driven by occupancy and rent gains across its East Coast markets that are expected to drive cash flow and earnings growth in 2026, with its West Coast markets expected to drive growth in 2027. West Coast markets have been slower to recover, but there are encouraging signs that fundamentals are bottoming.
  4. Attractive growth prospects over the next several years, as management believes the company can potentially grow earnings (FFO) mid-single digits, driven by occupancy gains (87% current versus 92% target), rent growth, and development lease-up, offset in part by debt refinancing headwinds.

At its current share price of $68, BXP is being valued at a 7.6% implied cap rate and less than $700 per square foot, which is a steep discount to private market values (5% to 6% cap rates) and replacement cost (well over $1,000 per square foot).

In the most recent quarter, we purchased additional shares in Equinix, Inc., the premier global operator of 270 network-dense, carrier-neutral colocation data centers with operations across 36 countries and 6 continents. We acquired shares at what we believed were compelling valuation levels. Shares retrenched, however, in the last few days of the quarter due to the company outlining incremental capital investments at its bi-annual Investor Day that will depress near-term growth but pay dividends longer term.

Though we are encouraged by the expanding growth drivers for Equinix, management’s updated five-year earnings growth outlook was below investor expectations. While top-line growth is encouraging, the company is ramping up capital investments over the next several years, which will dampen per share cash flow growth over the next two years, in particular. This led to a material initial sell-off in the shares. While the near-term growth prospects are disappointing and below our expectations as well, we believe the company is taking the right steps to position the business for higher growth ahead. Given Equinix needs to bring on new data center facilities to fulfill the demand signals they are seeing from customers, there is an initial drag on earnings while the data center is built and then stabilized. We bucket our current view of Equinix in the Baron investment framework where the company is taking “short-term pain for long-term gain.” Equinix has built a highly valuable inter-connected ecosystem and thus enjoys premium pricing and outsized returns on capital. Furthermore, the balance sheet remains well-positioned to fund this investment with ample debt capacity and no need for external equity.

Top net sales for the quarter

Quarter End Market Cap or Market Cap When Sold ($B) Net Amount Sold ($M)
American Tower Corporation (AMT) 103.5 26.7
AvalonBay Communities , Inc. (AVB) 29.1 25.1
Extra Space Storage Inc. (EXR) 31.8 23.7
Equity Residential (EQR) 25.6 23.6
American Homes 4 Rent (AMH) 13.4 21.8

American Tower Corporation was one of the Fund’s top purchases early in the first quarter. Shares performed well and in a more compressed timeline than we had anticipated. We modestly reduced our investment to manage overall position sizing while sourcing capital for other investment opportunities. American Tower is a global owner of 150,000 wireless tower communication sites with a heavy emphasis on developed markets. Our optimism regarding the long-term growth prospects for American Tower remain unchanged given strong secular growth expectations for mobile data usage, 5G spectrum deployment and network densification (with 6G around the corner), edge computing (possible requirement of mini data centers next to a tower presents an additional revenue opportunity), and growth in connected Internet of Things devices (e.g. homes and cars), which will require more wireless bandwidth usage and continued increased spending by the mobile carriers.

We exited AvalonBay Communities, Inc. during the second quarter due to less favorable economics for development along with near-term dilutive portfolio reshaping and modest growth prospects. We reallocated capital to higher conviction investment ideas.

We also sold our position in Extra Space Storage Inc. during the quarter. While we are long-term bullish on the prospects for self-storage real estate (excellent real estate businesses with less cyclical demand, pricing power, low capital intensity, scale advantages), we are concerned that lackluster existing home sales caused by the well-known “lock in effect” may weigh on self-storage demand and market rents for longer than is widely believed. We view current share price valuations as fair, not overly cheap. As such, for now we opted to reallocate capital to other investments with superior return potential. We may revisit Extra Space Storage, a best-in-class company, in the future.
